Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

SpTU 3, 079 [Namburbu][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
o 1o 1

NAM.BUR₂.BI ḪUL NITA u MUNUS SU₃-MEŠ ana a-ḫa-meš qur-ru-bu

(o 1) Apotropaic ritual for the evil (that may arise) in bringing to each other a man and woman (previously) far apart:

o 22

ana IGI dUTU <ina> KI KU₅si <KI> SAR A KU₃ SU₃ GI.DU₈ ana IGI dUTU u dASAR.LU₂.ḪI

(o 2) In front of Šamaš, in a private place, you sweep the ground . You sprinkle pure water. You set up an offering table before Šamaš and Asalluhi. You strew dates (and) fine flour. You put down a cake (made) of honey (and) butter. You set up a libation-vessel. You strew a censer (full) of juniper. You libate beer.

o 33

GUBan ZU₂.LUM.MA zi₃EŠA DUBaq NINDA.I₃.DE₂.A LAL₃ I₃.NUN.NA

o 44

GARan dugA.DA.GUR₅ GUBan NIG₂.NA šimLI DUBaq KAŠ BALqi₂

o 55

tug₂ŠA₃.ḪAa ina bi-rit-šu₂-nu GID₂ad 3 dugLA.ḪA.AN SAḪAR₂-MEŠ

(o 5) You hang a linen cloth in between them (the man and the woman). 3 porous flasks are smashed in between them and you speak as follows 3 times before Šamaš and Asalluhi.

o 66

ina bi-ri-šu₂-nu GAZ-MEŠ-ma IGI dUTU u dASAR.LU₂.ḪI 3-šu₂ kam DU₁₁.GA

o 77

dUTU ed-de-šu-u₂ ZALAG₂ DINGIR-MEŠ ka-a-a-nu dAMAR.UTU mu-šim NAM-MEŠ

(o 7) Self-renewing Šamaš, constant light of the gods, (and) Marduk, decreer of fates, lengthener of short days, gatherer of the scattered people, you are the ones who bring together the far apart.

o 88

mu-rik U₄-MEŠ LUGUD!(E₂)DA!(BIR-)MEŠ mu-paḫ-ḫir UN-MEŠ BIRaḫ

o 99

mu-qer₅-ru-bu a-ḫa-meš SU₃-⸢MEŠ⸣ [at]-⸢tu⸣-nu-ma mu-un-nar-bi

(o 9) Šamaš and Marduk, you are the ones bringing fugitives (and) runaways to one another. [...] giving a young man [...] each other,

o 1010

mu-un-nab!(AZ-)ti a-na ⸢a⸣-[ḫa-meš qur]-⸢ru⸣-bu dUTU u dAMAR.UTU

o 1111

at-tu-nu-ma ⸢lu₂?⸣[GURUŠ ... a-ḫa]-⸢meš ša₂⸣-ra-ku [x]

(rest of obverse missing)
Reverse
rr NaN  (start of reverse missing)
(rest of reverse blank)
